title: quercus robur subsp. cuneifolia
intro: Quercus robur subsp. cuneifolia, also known as the English oak, is a subspecies of the oak tree that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been used to treat various health issues due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus robur subsp. cuneifolia include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against cardiovascular disease, cancer, and other chronic conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
